attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	## In Progress
7	
8	A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
9	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
10	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
11	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
12	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
13	
14	## Todo
15	
16	A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
17	highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
18	Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
19	in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
20	
21	Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
22	markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
23	renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
24	1. **Ops layer / CLI decoupling — sweep remaining
25	   subcommands.** Init ported in 0.44.0 as the worked
26	   example (`Context` + `InitParams`, `From<&InitArgs>`
27	   at the binary edge, op body reads `params.account`
28	   not `args.account.account`). Remaining: sync, chid,
29	   push, clone, desc, fix-desc, validate-desc, list,
30	   show, finalize, symlink — each its own cycle, same
31	   shape (`<Mod>Params` + `From<&XArgs>`). [80]
32	1. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
33	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
34	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
35	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
36	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
37	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
38	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
39	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
40	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
41	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
42	   hardens. [82],[83]
43	1. **Rebase note — CLAUDE.md `### Per-file review checkpoints`.**
44	   Both `main` (0.42.0 work) and `init-clone-refactor`
45	   (0.41.1) authored this subsection independently —
46	   same intent, different wording. When 0.42.0 rebases on
47	   top of 0.41.1 at close-out, resolve CAREFULLY: don't
48	   take either side wholesale, reconcile to preserve the
49	   best of both. Likely conflict surface is the bullet
50	   list under "How to apply".
51	1. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
52	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
53	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
54	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
55	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
56	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
57	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
58	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
59	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [75]
60	1. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
61	   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
62	   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
63	   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
64	   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [73]
65	1. **`--scope` sweep continuation: finalize + Single(_)
66	   dogfood.** Carry-over from 0.42.0 cycle (closed at -4.7;
67	   -5/-7 deferred). Two pieces: `vc-x1 finalize --scope`
68	   replacing `--repo` (was 0.42.0-5), and `Single(_)`
69	   end-to-end dogfood validation (was 0.42.0-7). The third
70	   originally-paired item, `vc-x1 clone --scope` (was
71	   0.42.0-6), is tracked in its own entry below. Design
72	   lives in chores-07. [76]
73	1. **init dual|por arg split.** Via `#[command(flatten)]` of
74	   `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` (built in -6.7) +
75	   `provision_side(role, …)` shared helper. CLI surface
76	   decision (subcommands `init dual|por` vs preserved
77	   `--scope` flag with manual two-pass parse) deferred to
78	   design time. Was `0.41.1-6.9`; may or may not happen.
79	1. Decide merge direction for `init-clone-refactor`
80	   (recovered 2026-05-02). Rebase `0.41.1` onto
81	   `0.42.0` → `0.43.0`-or-`0.42.1`, vs force-rewrite
82	   main, vs other topology. [78]
83	1. Cross-file chores-NN ordering sanity pass at
84	   `init-clone-refactor` merge time. Bring chores-08.md
85	   (0.41.1 per-step subsections) onto main alongside
86	   chores-06/07; normalize section ordering across all
87	   three. Tied to merge-direction decision above. [78]
88	1. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
89	1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
90	   Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
91	   rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
92	   becomes scope-aware (single-side path skips
93	   `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`;
94	   `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
95	   [57],[60],[71],[72]
96	1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
97	   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
98	   via the path form. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
99	   close-out). [60],[71],[72]
100	1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
101	   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
102	   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
103	   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
104	   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
105	   two repos by definition). [60],[71],[72]
106	1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
107	   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
108	   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
109	   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
110	   the new path form. [60],[71],[76]
111	1. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
112	   (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
113	   `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
114	   Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
115	   `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
116	   `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
117	   Candidate for 0.41.2. [74]
118	1. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
119	   once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
120	   override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
121	   init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
122	   post-init commands can. Depends on the
123	   `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
124	   Candidate for 0.41.2. [74]
125	1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
126	   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
127	   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
128	   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
129	   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
130	   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
131	   visual inconsistency.
132	1. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
133	   `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
134	   `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
135	   under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
136	   Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
137	1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
138	   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
139	   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
140	   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
141	   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
142	   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
143	   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
144	   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
145	   sweep.
146	1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
147	   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
148	   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
149	   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
150	1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
151	   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
152	   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
153	   2026-04-24).
154	1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
155	   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
156	   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
157	   place first. [57]
158	1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
159	   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
160	   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
161	1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
162	   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
163	   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
164	1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
165	   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
166	   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
167	   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
168	   failed checks. Implementation: promote
169	   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
170	   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
171	1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
172	   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
173	   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
174	1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
175	   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
176	   differs from entry. [62]
177	1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
178	   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
179	   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
180	1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
181	   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
182	   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
183	   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
184	   needed for the new layout.
185	1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
186	   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
187	   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
188	   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
189	   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
190	1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
191	1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
192	1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
193	1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
194	1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
195	1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
196	1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
197	1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
198	1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]
199	1. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
200	   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
201	   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
202	   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
203	   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
204	   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
205	   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
206	   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
207	   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
208	   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
209	   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
210	   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
211	   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
212	   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
213	   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
214	   it adds value over the resolution chain.
215	
216	## Done
217	
218	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
219	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
220	
221	- Substep protocol formalization + jj revsets cheatsheet (0.42.0-4.5) [77]
222	- init-clone-refactor recovery + post-mortem playbook (0.42.0-4.6) [78]
223	- Init-clone-refactor rebase landing — main rebased + .claude re-paired (0.42.0-4.7) [79]
224	- 0.42.0 cycle close-out at -4.7 — partial --scope sweep, continuation deferred [81]
225	- Test-module extraction across init/push/sync/common (0.43.0) [84]
226	- InitParams + Context — init op decoupling worked example (0.44.0) [80]
227

highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. **Ops layer / CLI decoupling — sweep remaining
   subcommands.** Init ported in 0.44.0 as the worked
   example (`Context` + `InitParams`, `From<&InitArgs>`
   at the binary edge, op body reads `params.account`
   not `args.account.account`). Remaining: sync, chid,
   push, clone, desc, fix-desc, validate-desc, list,
   show, finalize, symlink — each its own cycle, same
   shape (`<Mod>Params` + `From<&XArgs>`). [80]
1. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [82],[83]
1. **Rebase note — CLAUDE.md `### Per-file review checkpoints`.**
   Both `main` (0.42.0 work) and `init-clone-refactor`
   (0.41.1) authored this subsection independently —
   same intent, different wording. When 0.42.0 rebases on
   top of 0.41.1 at close-out, resolve CAREFULLY: don't
   take either side wholesale, reconcile to preserve the
   best of both. Likely conflict surface is the bullet
   list under "How to apply".
1. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [75]
1. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [73]
1. **`--scope` sweep continuation: finalize + Single(_)
   dogfood.** Carry-over from 0.42.0 cycle (closed at -4.7;
   -5/-7 deferred). Two pieces: `vc-x1 finalize --scope`
   replacing `--repo` (was 0.42.0-5), and `Single(_)`
   end-to-end dogfood validation (was 0.42.0-7). The third
   originally-paired item, `vc-x1 clone --scope` (was
   0.42.0-6), is tracked in its own entry below. Design
   lives in chores-07. [76]
1. **init dual|por arg split.** Via `#[command(flatten)]` of
   `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` (built in -6.7) +
   `provision_side(role, …)` shared helper. CLI surface
   decision (subcommands `init dual|por` vs preserved
   `--scope` flag with manual two-pass parse) deferred to
   design time. Was `0.41.1-6.9`; may or may not happen.
1. Decide merge direction for `init-clone-refactor`
   (recovered 2026-05-02). Rebase `0.41.1` onto
   `0.42.0` → `0.43.0`-or-`0.42.1`, vs force-rewrite
   main, vs other topology. [78]
1. Cross-file chores-NN ordering sanity pass at
   `init-clone-refactor` merge time. Bring chores-08.md
   (0.41.1 per-step subsections) onto main alongside
   chores-06/07; normalize section ordering across all
   three. Tied to merge-direction decision above. [78]
1. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
   rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
   becomes scope-aware (single-side path skips
   `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`;
   `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
   close-out). [60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). [60],[71],[72]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. [60],[71],[76]
1. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
   (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
   `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
   Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
   `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
   `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
   Candidate for 0.41.2. [74]
1. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
   once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
   override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
   init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
   post-init commands can. Depends on the
   `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
   Candidate for 0.41.2. [74]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
   `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
   `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
   under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
   Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]
1. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
   it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Substep protocol formalization + jj revsets cheatsheet (0.42.0-4.5) [77]
- init-clone-refactor recovery + post-mortem playbook (0.42.0-4.6) [78]
- Init-clone-refactor rebase landing — main rebased + .claude re-paired (0.42.0-4.7) [79]
- 0.42.0 cycle close-out at -4.7 — partial --scope sweep, continuation deferred [81]
- Test-module extraction across init/push/sync/common (0.43.0) [84]
- InitParams + Context — init op decoupling worked example (0.44.0) [80]

# Chores-09.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..08.md;
09 starts here because chores-07 (0.42.0 cycle, 600+ lines) and
chores-08 (0.41.1 cycle, 1500+ lines) are both already large; the
init-clone-refactor rebase landing is a natural new-file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.

## init-clone-refactor rebase landing (0.42.0-4.7)

Rebased main (0.42.0-0..-4.6) onto init-clone-refactor at its
0.41.1 close-out tip (`slvprlpw`). Chids preserved; ochid
trailers in the rebased commits stayed structurally valid (the
.claude pairings they point at had been WC-only during the icr
divergence; -4.7 itself re-establishes the .claude side).

### What needed real work

- **ttzwvpoq (0.42.0-1):** full `Scope` enum lifted wholesale
  from pre-rebase main; `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `is_bot_only`
  and `Single` / `parse_scope` since icr's bundle design keeps
  them dormant.
- **mvusyowm (0.42.0-2):** icr's bundle-flatten `InitArgs` +
  `ScopeOption`/`ScopeKind` already supersedes mvusyowm's
  parser-switch + `Option<Scope>` design; took icr wholesale
  across all 5 init.rs conflict regions. The cycle's narrative
  intent (custom `--scope` parser accepting paths) becomes
  dormant on the icr base; the `Scope::Single` variant survives
  for future use.
- **kyurxpnu (0.42.0-4.5):** CLAUDE.md substep references merged
  (lulqxovr's icr-side decomposition pointer + kyurxpnu's
  external `substep-protocol.md` / `jj-revsets.md` links) into
  a single continuation paragraph under `### Versioning`.
- **Reference renumbering** — cycle's `[72]`/`[73]`/`[74]`
  collided with icr's chores-08 anchors: `[72]→[76]`,
  `[73]→[77]`, `[74]→[78]`, plus self-refs in chores-07's
  -4.5 and -4.6 sections.

### Precautions taken (most unnecessary in retrospect)

- `main-2` local duplicate bookmark — never read.
- `gca-icr-main` common-ancestor marker — never referenced.
- `rslv-commit` cursor bookmark — handy as a navigation
  aid during cascade, not load-bearing (`@-` works).
- `../vc-x1-main` + `../vc-x1-icr` reference clones — useful
  for content lookup at specific chids; replaceable with
  `jj log -r <chid> --patch` against the local repo.
- Filesystem snapshots `vc-x1-20260505-1`, `vc-x1-20260506-1`
  — never restored from.
- `~/vc-x1-rebase-status.md` scratch file — useful for
  resuming across sessions, but ~200 lines was over-detailed;
  a 30-line cursor + decisions log would have sufficed.

### What was load-bearing

- `jj op restore` for local rewinds.
...

content.content:
506	## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)
507	
508	Design target for subsequent cycles: separate clap-aware
509	parsing from subcommand operation logic so future front-ends
510	(e.g. a TUI for bot-conversation exploration, or library
511	embedding) call the same core without retrofitting. Captures
512	the conclusion of the 0.42.0-4.7 side discussion about
513	`args.account.account` ergonomics — renaming / accessor
514	shortcuts were rejected as hiding the architecture mismatch
515	rather than fixing it.
516	
517	### Goals
518	
519	- Subcommand bodies access flat fields (`opts.account`), not
520	  nested clap shapes (`args.account.account`).
521	- `src/options_flags/` leaves remain the single source of
522	  truth for flag definition (parser, help, completer).
523	- Per-subcommand `-h` stays bundle-specific.
524	- Tab completion (`vc-x1 init <TAB>`, `--account=<TAB>`)
525	  keeps working via clap_complete + dynamic completers.
526	- The ops layer is callable without clap as a dependency
527	  (today's `plan_init(args: &InitArgs, ...)` is not).
528	
529	### Two layers, three structures per subcommand
530	
531	- **CLI layer (clap-aware):** `InitArgs` / `CloneArgs` / etc.
532	  `#[derive(Args)]` types that flatten leaves from
533	  `src/options_flags/`. Own clap metadata for `-h` and
534	  completion. Live at the binary edge.
535	- **Ops layer (clap-free):** `InitOptions` / `CloneOptions`
536	  — plain structs, flat fields, `Default`. Plus `Workspace`,
537	  the shared platform handle (workspace root, loaded
538	  `UserConfig`, optional progress sink). Entrypoints are
539	  `ops::init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions) -> Result<InitOutcome, InitError>`.
540	- **Boundary conversion:** `From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions`
541	  — one contained site per subcommand where leaf nesting is
542	  unpacked. Op bodies never see `args.xxx.xxx`.
543	
544	### `Workspace` ("context") vs per-op options
545	
546	- **`Workspace`:** shared platform every op runs against —
547	  workspace root, loaded user config, optional progress
548	  sink. Same shape across subcommands.
549	- **`InitOptions`** etc.: per-op input; shape differs by
550	  subcommand.
551	
552	Two parameters, not a single merged "god context." The
553	signature `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`
554	documents what the op depends on; a merged `Context`
555	containing every possible field would let any op silently
556	read any field with no signature-level visibility.
557	
558	If `Workspace` itself grows, escape valves (defer until
559	needed):
560	
561	- Split into `Workspace` (paths + config, read-only, cheap)
562	  vs `Session` (progress / cancellation, mutable). Most ops
563	  want `&Workspace`; long-running ops also accept
564	  `&Session`.
565	- Trait-based DI (`fn init<W: HasConfig + HasFs>(...)`) for
566	  multiple front-ends with genuinely different platform
567	  surfaces. Heavy in Rust; not the default.
568	
569	### Five rules for the ops layer
570	
571	1. **Plain options, flat fields, `Default`.** No clap types
572	   in `InitOptions`. Domain values like `RepoSelector` are
573	   fine (they're domain types); leaf wrappers like
574	   `RepoOption` are not.
575	2. **Typed errors.** `enum InitError` (likely via
576	   `thiserror`), not `Box<dyn Error>`. A TUI matches
577	   variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
578	   `Box<dyn Error>` discards exactly the information a GUI
579	   most needs.
580	3. **Returned outcomes, not `println!`.** Each op returns a
581	   structured result; CLI formats it for stdout, TUI
582	   populates panels. Library writes nothing to stdout
583	   itself.
584	4. **Progress via a sink.** Long-running ops accept an
585	   optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` (or
586	   `mpsc::Sender<Event>`). CLI installs a stderr sink; TUI
587	   a status-bar sink; tests a recording sink. `log` /
588	   `tracing` covers diagnostics but is lossy for structured
589	   progress.
590	5. **No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops.**
591	   Everything an op needs goes in `Workspace` or
592	   `*Options`. CLI resolves cwd/env once at startup and
593	   builds the handle. This is what lets a multi-window TUI
594	   drive multiple workspaces without cross-contamination.
595	
596	### Completion stays at the clap layer
597	
598	- Static structure (`-h`, subcommand layout, flag presence):
599	  clap derive on `InitArgs` + leaves. Unchanged from today.
600	- Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>` against
601	  accounts in user config): `ArgValueCompleter` attached to
602	  the leaf in `src/options_flags/`. Already enabled via
603	  `clap_complete`'s `unstable-dynamic` feature. The leaf is
604	  the right home — completion is a clap-aware concern with
605	  no place in `InitOptions`.
606	
607	Ops layer stays clap-free; leaf layer stays clap-aware.
608	Completion drives nothing in the ops layer.
609	
610	### The contained wart
611	
612	`From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions` walks leaf nesting once
613	per subcommand:
614	
615	```rust
616	impl From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions {
617	    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
618	        Self {
619	            target: a.target.clone(),
620	            account: a.account.account.clone(),
621	            scope: a.scope.scope,
622	            private: a.provision.private.private,
623	            // ...
624	        }
625	    }
626	}
627	```
628	
629	Verbose but contained: one site per subcommand. Adding a new
630	flag becomes a two-edit change (clap struct + options struct
631	+ one conversion line). Accepted price of decoupling; bodies
632	are flat thereafter.
633	
634	### What this is *not*
635	
636	- **Not a god `Context`.** Two parameters
637	  (`&Workspace`, `&XOptions`), not one merged blob.
638	- **Not premature trait-based DI.** Concrete `Workspace` +
639	  `*Options` structs until a second front-end forces
640	  generalization.
641	- **Not a crate split.** Same crate, separate modules
642	  (e.g. `src/ops/init.rs` vs CLI edge). Promote to a
643	  workspace only when a second consumer crate appears.
644	- **Not removing `src/options_flags/`.** Leaves stay; only
645	  their consumers change shape.
646	
647	### Migration sketch
648	
649	The bot thinks the safest first move is converting one
650	subcommand end-to-end as the worked example before any
651	sweep. `init` is the largest surface; `sync` or `chid` would
652	be a lighter proof of concept.
653	
654	1. Introduce `ops::Workspace` (paths + `UserConfig` only; no
655	   progress sink yet). Wire it into one subcommand.
656	2. Introduce `XOptions` for the chosen first subcommand;
657	   write `From<&XArgs> for XOptions`; port the body to
658	   `&Workspace, &XOptions`.
659	3. Add typed errors + returned outcome for that subcommand.
660	4. Sweep remaining subcommands; each is a contained step.
661	5. (Later) introduce `ProgressSink` when a TUI need actually
662	   surfaces, or a long-running op wants structured progress.
663	
664	The bot thinks the right shape of `Workspace` matters more
665	than the right shape of `InitError` — defer error-type and
666	outcome-type design until step 1 has shaken out.
667	
668	### Open questions
669	
670	- `Workspace` carries the progress sink from day one even
671	  with no consumer? The bot's guess is no — leave it out and
672	  add when a real long-running op forces it; adding later is
673	  mechanical.
674	- `XOptions` owns values (`String`) or borrows (`&str`)? The
675	  bot's guess is owns — simpler, matches the programmatic-
676	  caller path (build then call). Defer borrow optimization
677	  until benchmarks call for it.
678	- Crate split timing — defer until a second consumer crate
679	  appears.
680	
681	## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)
682	
683	Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
684	port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
685	example for the design captured in
686	[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
687	above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.
688	
689	### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above
690	
691	Written before this cycle, the design section uses
692	`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:
693	
694	- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
695	  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
696	  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
697	  Doubly overloaded.
698	- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
699	  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
700	  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
701	  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.
702	
703	The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
704	this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
705	is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
706	during a future close-out so the codebase has one
707	naming.
708	
709	### Why init
710	
711	`args.account.account` ergonomics — the trigger for the
712	whole design — lives here; init also has the deepest
713	leaf nesting (account / repo / scope / private /
714	template / push retry). If the pattern survives init,
715	sync / chid / push / clone port trivially.
716	
717	### In scope
718	
719	- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding the
720	  workspace root path + loaded `UserConfig`. Built once
721	  at startup.
722	- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat plain
723	  struct (owns its values) + `impl From<&InitArgs> for
724	  InitParams`.
725	- `src/init.rs`: port `plan_init` and any other
726	  init-side `pub fn` entrypoints to
727	  `fn (&Context, &InitParams)`. Body reads
728	  `params.account` instead of `args.account.account`.
729	- `src/main.rs`: build `Context` once at startup; the
730	  init dispatch arm passes `(&ctx, &(&args).into())`.
731	- `src/init/tests.rs`: pure-unit tests construct
732	  `Context` + `InitParams` directly (no clap
733	  `try_parse_from` plumbing); integration paths still
734	  exercise the clap edge.
735	
736	### Out of scope (deferred)
737	
738	- Sweep across sync / chid / push / clone / etc.
739	  Each is its own later cycle.
740	- Per chores-09 "five rules": typed errors, returned
741	  outcomes (vs `println!`), `ProgressSink`. Keep this
742	  cycle to the structural split only.
743	- `Context` fields beyond workspace root + user
744	  config. Defer until a real consumer surfaces.
745	
746	### Pre-commit checklist
747	
748	Standard: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --
749	-D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
750	--locked`, retest.
751	
752	### Edits
753	
754	- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
755	- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding
756	  `user_config: UserConfig` + `Context::load()` ctor;
757	  declared `mod context;` in `src/main.rs`.
758	- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat struct
759	  + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production
760	  default sets `create_symlink: true`); declared
761	  `mod params; pub use params::InitParams;` in
762	  `src/init.rs`.
763	- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` signature changed
764	  from `init(args: &InitArgs, create_symlink: bool)`
765	  to `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`
766	  (`create_symlink` folded into `InitParams`).
767	  Internal helpers (`plan_init`, `plan_from_url`,
768	  `plan_from_path`, `plan_from_bare_name`,
769	  `plan_remote`, `plan_local`, `build_plan`,
770	  `create_por`, `create_dual`, `push_repo`,
771	  `run_remote_step`) all switched from
772	  `args: &InitArgs` to `params: &InitParams`. Body
773	  reads `params.account` instead of
774	  `args.account.account` (and similarly across all
775	  leaf nestings). `args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)`

file.content:
## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)

Design target for subsequent cycles: separate clap-aware
parsing from subcommand operation logic so future front-ends
(e.g. a TUI for bot-conversation exploration, or library
embedding) call the same core without retrofitting. Captures
the conclusion of the 0.42.0-4.7 side discussion about
`args.account.account` ergonomics — renaming / accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the architecture mismatch
rather than fixing it.

### Goals

- Subcommand bodies access flat fields (`opts.account`), not
  nested clap shapes (`args.account.account`).
- `src/options_flags/` leaves remain the single source of
  truth for flag definition (parser, help, completer).
- Per-subcommand `-h` stays bundle-specific.
- Tab completion (`vc-x1 init <TAB>`, `--account=<TAB>`)
  keeps working via clap_complete + dynamic completers.
- The ops layer is callable without clap as a dependency
  (today's `plan_init(args: &InitArgs, ...)` is not).

### Two layers, three structures per subcommand

- **CLI layer (clap-aware):** `InitArgs` / `CloneArgs` / etc.
  `#[derive(Args)]` types that flatten leaves from
  `src/options_flags/`. Own clap metadata for `-h` and
  completion. Live at the binary edge.
- **Ops layer (clap-free):** `InitOptions` / `CloneOptions`
  — plain structs, flat fields, `Default`. Plus `Workspace`,
  the shared platform handle (workspace root, loaded
  `UserConfig`, optional progress sink). Entrypoints are
  `ops::init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions) -> Result<InitOutcome, InitError>`.
- **Boundary conversion:** `From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions`
  — one contained site per subcommand where leaf nesting is
  unpacked. Op bodies never see `args.xxx.xxx`.

### `Workspace` ("context") vs per-op options

- **`Workspace`:** shared platform every op runs against —
  workspace root, loaded user config, optional progress
  sink. Same shape across subcommands.
- **`InitOptions`** etc.: per-op input; shape differs by
  subcommand.

Two parameters, not a single merged "god context." The
signature `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`
documents what the op depends on; a merged `Context`
containing every possible field would let any op silently
read any field with no signature-level visibility.

If `Workspace` itself grows, escape valves (defer until
needed):

- Split into `Workspace` (paths + config, read-only, cheap)
  vs `Session` (progress / cancellation, mutable). Most ops
  want `&Workspace`; long-running ops also accept
  `&Session`.
- Trait-based DI (`fn init<W: HasConfig + HasFs>(...)`) for
  multiple front-ends with genuinely different platform
  surfaces. Heavy in Rust; not the default.

### Five rules for the ops layer

1. **Plain options, flat fields, `Default`.** No clap types
   in `InitOptions`. Domain values like `RepoSelector` are
   fine (they're domain types); leaf wrappers like
   `RepoOption` are not.
2. **Typed errors.** `enum InitError` (likely via
   `thiserror`), not `Box<dyn Error>`. A TUI matches
   variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
   `Box<dyn Error>` discards exactly the information a GUI
   most needs.
3. **Returned outcomes, not `println!`.** Each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it for stdout, TUI
   populates panels. Library writes nothing to stdout
   itself.
4. **Progress via a sink.** Long-running ops accept an
   optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` (or
   `mpsc::Sender<Event>`). CLI installs a stderr sink; TUI
   a status-bar sink; tests a recording sink. `log` /
   `tracing` covers diagnostics but is lossy for structured
   progress.
5. **No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops.**
   Everything an op needs goes in `Workspace` or
   `*Options`. CLI resolves cwd/env once at startup and
   builds the handle. This is what lets a multi-window TUI
   drive multiple workspaces without cross-contamination.

### Completion stays at the clap layer

- Static structure (`-h`, subcommand layout, flag presence):
  clap derive on `InitArgs` + leaves. Unchanged from today.
- Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>` against
  accounts in user config): `ArgValueCompleter` attached to
  the leaf in `src/options_flags/`. Already enabled via
  `clap_complete`'s `unstable-dynamic` feature. The leaf is
  the right home — completion is a clap-aware concern with
  no place in `InitOptions`.

Ops layer stays clap-free; leaf layer stays clap-aware.
Completion drives nothing in the ops layer.

### The contained wart

`From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions` walks leaf nesting once
per subcommand:

```rust
impl From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions {
    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
        Self {
            target: a.target.clone(),
            account: a.account.account.clone(),
            scope: a.scope.scope,
            private: a.provision.private.private,
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

Verbose but contained: one site per subcommand. Adding a new
flag becomes a two-edit change (clap struct + options struct
+ one conversion line). Accepted price of decoupling; bodies
are flat thereafter.

### What this is *not*

- **Not a god `Context`.** Two parameters
  (`&Workspace`, `&XOptions`), not one merged blob.
- **Not premature trait-based DI.** Concrete `Workspace` +
  `*Options` structs until a second front-end forces
  generalization.
- **Not a crate split.** Same crate, separate modules
  (e.g. `src/ops/init.rs` vs CLI edge). Promote to a
  workspace only when a second consumer crate appears.
- **Not removing `src/options_flags/`.** Leaves stay; only
  their consumers change shape.

### Migration sketch

The bot thinks the safest first move is converting one
subcommand end-to-end as the worked example before any
sweep. `init` is the largest surface; `sync` or `chid` would
be a lighter proof of concept.

1. Introduce `ops::Workspace` (paths + `UserConfig` only; no
   progress sink yet). Wire it into one subcommand.
2. Introduce `XOptions` for the chosen first subcommand;
   write `From<&XArgs> for XOptions`; port the body to
   `&Workspace, &XOptions`.
3. Add typed errors + returned outcome for that subcommand.
4. Sweep remaining subcommands; each is a contained step.
5. (Later) introduce `ProgressSink` when a TUI need actually
   surfaces, or a long-running op wants structured progress.

The bot thinks the right shape of `Workspace` matters more
than the right shape of `InitError` — defer error-type and
outcome-type design until step 1 has shaken out.

### Open questions

- `Workspace` carries the progress sink from day one even
  with no consumer? The bot's guess is no — leave it out and
  add when a real long-running op forces it; adding later is
  mechanical.
- `XOptions` owns values (`String`) or borrows (`&str`)? The
  bot's guess is owns — simpler, matches the programmatic-
  caller path (build then call). Defer borrow optimization
  until benchmarks call for it.
- Crate split timing — defer until a second consumer crate
  appears.

## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)

Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
example for the design captured in
[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.

### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above

Written before this cycle, the design section uses
`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:

- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
  Doubly overloaded.
- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.

The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
during a future close-out so the codebase has one
naming.

### Why init

`args.account.account` ergonomics — the trigger for the
whole design — lives here; init also has the deepest
leaf nesting (account / repo / scope / private /
template / push retry). If the pattern survives init,
sync / chid / push / clone port trivially.

### In scope

- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding the
  workspace root path + loaded `UserConfig`. Built once
  at startup.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat plain
  struct (owns its values) + `impl From<&InitArgs> for
  InitParams`.
- `src/init.rs`: port `plan_init` and any other
  init-side `pub fn` entrypoints to
  `fn (&Context, &InitParams)`. Body reads
  `params.account` instead of `args.account.account`.
- `src/main.rs`: build `Context` once at startup; the
  init dispatch arm passes `(&ctx, &(&args).into())`.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: pure-unit tests construct
  `Context` + `InitParams` directly (no clap
  `try_parse_from` plumbing); integration paths still
  exercise the clap edge.

### Out of scope (deferred)

- Sweep across sync / chid / push / clone / etc.
  Each is its own later cycle.
- Per chores-09 "five rules": typed errors, returned
  outcomes (vs `println!`), `ProgressSink`. Keep this
  cycle to the structural split only.
- `Context` fields beyond workspace root + user
  config. Defer until a real consumer surfaces.

### Pre-commit checklist

Standard: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --
-D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
--locked`, retest.

### Edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding
  `user_config: UserConfig` + `Context::load()` ctor;
  declared `mod context;` in `src/main.rs`.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat struct
  + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production
  default sets `create_symlink: true`); declared
  `mod params; pub use params::InitParams;` in
  `src/init.rs`.
- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` signature changed
  from `init(args: &InitArgs, create_symlink: bool)`
  to `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`
  (`create_symlink` folded into `InitParams`).
  Internal helpers (`plan_init`, `plan_from_url`,
  `plan_from_path`, `plan_from_bare_name`,
  `plan_remote`, `plan_local`, `build_plan`,
  `create_por`, `create_dual`, `push_repo`,
  `run_remote_step`) all switched from
  `args: &InitArgs` to `params: &InitParams`. Body
  reads `params.account` instead of
  `args.account.account` (and similarly across all
  leaf nestings). `args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)`

content.text:
Acquainted. Here's where `vc-x1` stands.

**Project** — A Rust CLI (`vc-x1` v0.44.0, ~13.7k LOC) for managing a dual jj-git workspace: an app repo + a `.claude` bot-session repo, with synced commit messages (`ochid:` trailers), bookmark advancement, and a `push` command that wraps the commit→push→finalize flow with two approval gates. Subcommands: `init`, `clone`, `sync`, `chid`, `push`, `desc`/`fix-desc`/`validate-desc`, `list`, `show`, `finalize`, `symlink`.

**Repo state** — On `main` at `be7f2502` (0.44.0), working copy clean and empty. `.claude` has the usual live session-data churn. No in-progress multi-step cycle (`notes/todo.md > ## In Progress` is just the template stub).

**Last cycle (0.44.0)** — "Introduce Context + InitParams": the worked example for an *ops-layer decoupling* — separating clap-aware arg structs (`InitArgs`) from clap-free op input (`InitParams`, flat owned fields) plus a shared `Context` (workspace root + loaded `UserConfig`). Boundary conversion via `From<&InitArgs> for InitParams`. Goal: future front-ends (TUI, library embedding) call the core without clap. Naming note: the forward-looking design in `chores-09.md` says `Workspace`/`XOptions`; the implementation chose `Context`/`XParams` to dodge Cargo's "workspace" and Rust's `Option`.

**Next up (todo #1)** — Sweep the same `<Mod>Params` + `From<&XArgs>` pattern across the remaining subcommands (sync, chid, push, clone, desc, fix-desc, validate-desc, list, show, finalize, symlink) — each its own cycle. Other queued work: forks/multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through, `--scope` sweep continuation (finalize, clone, validate-desc), symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema, `test_helpers::Fixture` rename/migration, a `status`/`st` subcommand, `validate-repo` diagnostic.

What would you like to work on?

content: vc-x1 is decoupling clap arg-parsing from subcommand logic; the 0.44.0 worked example (Context + InitParams) just landed on main. Next action: pick a subcommand (sync or chid is lightest) and apply the same XParams + From<&XArgs> pattern as its own cycle. (disable recaps in /config)

  `Context` and `InitParams` into scope.

content.text:
Here's the lay of the land before I touch anything.

## What the cleanup actually is

In `notes/chores-09.md`, two spots use the pre-0.44.0 names:

1. **`## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)`** (lines ~506–679) — uses `Workspace` and `XOptions`/`InitOptions`/`CloneOptions` throughout, plus the subsection header ``### `Workspace` ("context") vs per-op options``, and the example signature `ops::init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`. All of that becomes `Context` / `XParams` / `InitParams` / ``### `Context` vs per-op params`` / `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`. The "Migration sketch" step "convert one subcommand end-to-end as the worked example" is now done (init, 0.44.0) — worth a one-line "✓ done in 0.44.0" note.

2. **`### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above`** (lines ~689–707) — once §1 is reconciled, this loses its reason to exist. I'd shrink it to a 2-line historical note keeping just the *why* (`Workspace`→`Context` because Cargo + `find_workspace_root` already own "workspace"; `Options`→`Params` to dodge `Option<T>`) and drop the "left as-is for this cycle / retitle during a future close-out" framing.

Scope check: other `Workspace*` hits in notes (`WorkspaceConfig`, `SingleRepoWorkspace`, jj-lib's `Workspace::load()` in chores-01, the `Workspace` handle in `bot-data-formats.md`) are unrelated concepts — leave them. `notes/todo.md` #1 already says "Context + InitParams", so it's fine.

## Squash into 0.44.0 vs a 0.44.1 doc cycle

**Status:** 0.44.0 is already pushed to `origin` in *both* repos (`main@origin` = `be7f2502` app / `c4a040a3` .claude). So this isn't a free local amend.

- **Squash into 0.44.0** — needs `jj squash --ignore-immutable -R .` + `jj git push --bookmark main -R .` (a force-push of already-published history), then clear `.vc-x1/push-state.toml` per the "out-of-band recovery" rule. Low-risk on a 2-day-old commit in a solo repo, but it's the exception path, not `vc-x1 push`. `.claude` wouldn't strictly need a force-push (change IDs survive squash, so ochid trailers stay valid) but you'd want a normal session commit there anyway. Also: chores-09's "Edits" list for 0.44.0 would need a small touch-up so it describes the squashed-in state.

- **0.44.1 doc-only cycle** — normal `vc-x1 push main`, two gates, no force-push, no state-file cleanup. One more version entry for a doc edit, but the project does that routinely (`0.21.1`, `0.22.1`, …). And chores-09 itself says the section "can be retitled / updated during a future close-out" — i.e. a follow-up was the anticipated path.

**My lean: 0.44.1.** The workflow is built around forward-only history, the notes already foreshadow a follow-up, and the force-push buys very little here. But it's close to a coin-flip — if you'd rather keep it folded in, the squash path is documented and safe in this case.

Which way do you want to go? (That decides whether step 1 is a version bump to `0.44.1` or a `--ignore-immutable` squash.)

content.text:
Good call on a standalone doc cycle — and yeah, there's a real gap: `README.md` is user-facing usage, `src/options_flags/README.md` covers the leaf/bundle micro-pattern, `CLAUDE.md` is bot workflow — but nothing describes the app's *structure* (module map, the Args↔Context/Params split, subcommand dispatch, what's mid-migration). Worth its own document.

Here's how I'd shape it.

## Cycle
- **0.45.0, single-step, doc-only.** Focused scope → direct bump, one commit (per CLAUDE.md's single-step guidance). Normal `vc-x1 push main` flow.

## New doc: `notes/architecture.md`
Matches the existing `notes/` design-doc convention (`forks-multi-user.md`, `bot-data-formats.md`, `substep-protocol.md`). (Alternative: root `ARCHITECTURE.md` — GitHub special-cases that filename for discoverability. I lean `notes/` for consistency, but flag it as a choice.)

Proposed outline:
1. **Overview** — one binary, `vc-x1 <subcommand>`, jj dual-repo tooling.
2. **Module map** — `src/main.rs` (clap entry + dispatch + banner walker), `src/options_flags/` (reusable leaves/bundles → link its README), per-subcommand modules, `src/context.rs`, `src/scope.rs`, support modules (`repo_utils`, `toml_simple`, `url`, `logging`, test helpers).
3. **Two layers** — CLI layer (clap-aware `XArgs`, flatten leaves) ↔ ops layer (clap-free `Context` + `XParams`), boundary `From<&XArgs> for XParams`. This absorbs the *normative* content currently in chores-09's "Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)" — the five rules, `Context`-vs-per-op-params, "what this is not", completion-stays-at-clap-layer.
4. **Subcommand model** — dispatch pattern, `cli_with_banner` walker, completion (`clap_complete` + `ArgValueCompleter` on leaves).
5. **In-flight migrations + status tables** — *two* separate ones:
   - (a) **Args → Context/Params** (ops-layer decoupling): init ✓ (0.44.0); sync/chid/push/clone/desc/fix-desc/validate-desc/list/show/finalize/symlink ✗.
   - (b) **Per-subcommand inline flag structs → `src/options_flags/` leaves**: leaves exist today for account/config/dry_run/private/provision_bundle/push_retry/repo/scope/use_template (mostly init's surface); the rest still inline. (I'll verify the exact state when I write it.)
   - The doc explains *the pattern and why*; the **live checklist stays in `notes/todo.md` #1** — no duplicated TODO lists.
6. **Cross-links** — pointer added from `notes/README.md`, `src/options_flags/README.md`, and root `README.md`'s Contributing section.

## chores-09 reconciliation — decision point
The "Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)" section was forward-looking *because the doc didn't exist*. Now it will. Two ways to handle it:

- **(A) Trim to a stub** — replace the section body with "Superseded by `notes/architecture.md` (0.45.0); originally captured the ops-layer design pre-implementation," keeping maybe the "Open questions" if any still have residual value. chores files stay a per-cycle journal; the living spec lives in one place.
- **(B) Leave it as a journal entry, just fix terminology** — `Workspace`/`XOptions` → `Context`/`XParams` so it doesn't contradict reality, but keep the prose. Downside: it then duplicates architecture.md and the two can drift.

I lean **(A)** — single source of truth. Either way, the `### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above` subsection collapses to a one-liner (keep just the *why*: `Workspace`→`Context` because Cargo + `find_workspace_root` own "workspace"; `Options`→`Params` to dodge `Option<T>`) or disappears.

## Two things I'd want your call on before I start
1. **Doc location** — `notes/architecture.md` (my pick) vs root `ARCHITECTURE.md`?
2. **chores-09 section** — (A) trim to stub (my pick) vs (B) keep + just fix terminology?

Once you pick those I'll do the version bump to 0.45.0 and draft the doc.

1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
7	about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
8	
9	In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
10	database to better manage the information, TBD.
11	
12	Examples chore file:
13	```
14	# Chores-01.md
15	 
16	General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
17	more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
18	how and why of a particular chore.
19	
20	## Create a binary that lists jj info 
21	
22	This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
23	and using `jj-lib`
24	```
25	
26	## Workflow and conventions
27	
28	Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
29	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:
30	
31	- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
32	  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
33	- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
34	  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
35	  stuck detection.
36	- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
37	  discipline with hard stop after finalize.
38	
39	## Todo format
40	
41	Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
42	short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
43	references.
44	
45	Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
46	markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
47	renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
48	work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
49	referenced by number once completed.
50	
51	Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
52	In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
53	is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).
54	
55	Examples:
56	
57	# Todo
58	- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
59	- Fix bug Y [1]
60	
61	# Done
62	- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]
63	
64	[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
65	[2]: issues.md#issue-z
66	[3]: fixes.md#fix-z
67	
68	## Reference numbering
69	
70	Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
71	file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
72	are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
73	`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
74	different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.
75	
76	## Retiring Done entries
77	
78	`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
79	work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
80	beats:
81	
82	- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
83	  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
84	  context and migrate them.
85	- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
86	  bumping the version.
87	
88	Migration mechanics:
89	
90	- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
91	  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
92	- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
93	  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
94	  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
95	- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
96	  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
97	  frees the numbers for future reuse.

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

20	This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
21	We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
22	in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
23	initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
24	made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
25	and one with the conversation with the bot.
26	
27	I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
28	each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
29	of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
30	changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
31	between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
32	evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
33	most importantly **why** the code evolved.
34	
35	The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
36	(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
37	existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
38	own IDE for vc.
39	
40	See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
41	for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
42	doing so and I then created this README.md file.
43

file.content:
# vc-x1

- [Overview](#vc-x1)
- [Usage](#usage)
  - [Revision shortcuts](#revision-shortcuts)
  - [Shell completion](#shell-completion)
  - [validate-desc](#validate-desc)
  - [fix-desc](#fix-desc)
  - [clone](#clone)
  - [init](#init)
  - [symlink](#symlink)
  - [finalize](#finalize)
  - [Testing push + finalize](#testing-push--finalize)
- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
- [Thoughts for the future](#thoughts-for-the-future)
- [Contributing](#contributing)
- [License](#license)

This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
and one with the conversation with the bot.

I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
most importantly **why** the code evolved.

The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
own IDE for vc.

See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
doing so and I then created this README.md file.

## Usage

content.content:
## Thoughts for the future

Forward-looking design discussion lives in
[`notes/forks-multi-user.md`](notes/forks-multi-user.md) —
forking the dual-repo workspace, multi-user collaboration,
multi-line `ochid:` trailers, bot-repo size and scaling
thresholds, URL-shaped per-user repos for distributed
projects. Treat as a design reference, not a status doc;
most of what's there is forward-looking, not yet
implemented.

## Contributing

Bot-following workflow, commit conventions, and code style are
canonical in [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md). Roughly in the order a
contributor encounters them:

- [Versioning](CLAUDE.md#versioning) — `-N` pre-release suffix
  convention (single-step vs multi-step); cross-links to
  Sub-step Workflow for finer depths.
- [Sub-step Workflow](CLAUDE.md#sub-step-workflow) — sub-step
  (`X.Y.Z-N.M`) / sub-sub-step (`X.Y.Z-N.M-K`) decomposition,
  per-sub-step cargo cycle, commit-first review model, ochid
  + `.claude` cadence, cycle close-out (squash vs keep
  separate), reviewing committed sub-steps.
- [Pre-commit Requirements](CLAUDE.md#pre-commit-requirements) —
  user approval gates, review-before-commit-block, per-file
  review checkpoints (with sub-step exception).
- [Pre-commit checklist](CLAUDE.md#pre-commit-checklist) — fmt
  / clippy / test / install + todo / chores / README updates.
- [Commit Message Style](CLAUDE.md#commit-message-style) —
  Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape per
  app vs `.claude` repo.
- [ochid Trailers](CLAUDE.md#ochid-trailers) — paired
  cross-references between app and `.claude` commits.
- [Commit-Push-Finalize Flow](CLAUDE.md#commit-push-finalize-flow) —
  `vc-x1 push` two-gate ceremony (review + message), preflight,
  finalize-claude.
- [Code Conventions](CLAUDE.md#code-conventions) — doc comments
  on every file / fn / method, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls,
  ask-on-ambiguity, stuck detection.

Task tracking and release details live under [notes/](notes/):
near-term tasks in [notes/todo.md](notes/todo.md), per-release
details in `notes/chores-*.md`, and notes-specific formatting
rules in [notes/README.md](notes/README.md).

- MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
