input.content:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer, the (emerging) clap-free ops layer, the subcommand
model, and the two in-flight migrations toward that shape.

This is a living document — when the migrations land, update
the status tables here. Per-cycle design discussion stays in
`notes/chores-*.md`; the canonical "current shape" lives
here.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary, `vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for
managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace** — an app repo plus
a `.claude` bot-session repo whose commits cross-reference
each other via `ochid:` trailers. See the root
[`README.md`](../README.md) for the user-facing picture and
[`CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md) for the bot workflow.

Each subcommand is a module under `src/`. `src/main.rs` owns
the clap command tree and dispatch; everything else is a
subcommand module or a shared support module.

## Module map

CLI edge:

- `src/main.rs` — `Cli` (`#[derive(Parser)]`) → `Commands`
  (`#[derive(Subcommand)]`) → per-variant dispatch arms.
  Also: `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand), `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion,
  and `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable CLI **leaves** and
  **bundles** (`account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`,
  `provision_bundle`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`,
  `use_template`) plus marker traits. The leaf/bundle
  composition patterns and the Flag-vs-Option classification
  are documented in
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (shared positional
  revision / `-R`,`--repo` list / `-n`,`--commits` /
  `--limit` / `-L`,`--no-label`) flattened by the read-only
  multi-repo subcommands (`chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`),
  plus the repo-iteration and revision-resolution helpers
  they share.

Ops-layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | ops-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Two layers: CLI args vs ops Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Ops layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters, not one merged "god context": the signature
documents what the op depends on. `Context` is the same
shape across subcommands; `XParams` differs per subcommand.

Rules for the ops layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running ops — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

What this is **not**: not a god `Context` (two params, not
one blob); not premature trait-based DI (concrete structs
until a second front-end forces it); not a crate split (same
crate, separate modules — promote to a workspace only when a
second consumer crate appears); not removing
`src/options_flags/` (leaves stay; only their consumers
change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Ops layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`todo.md`](todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`todo.md`](todo.md) are the usual entry point for
  converting one.

## See also

- [`../README.md`](../README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`../src/options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`todo.md`](todo.md) — live task list, including both
  migrations.
- [`chores-09.md`](chores-09.md) — the 0.43.0 / 0.44.0
  design capture this document supersedes.

First unit done — `Cargo.toml` bumped to **0.45.0**, and **`notes/architecture.md`** written (new file). Structure:

- **Overview** — single binary, dual-repo model, pointers to README/CLAUDE.
- **Module map** — CLI edge (`main.rs`, `options_flags/`, `common.rs`), ops-layer scaffolding (`context.rs`, `config.rs`, `scope.rs`), a table of the 12 subcommand modules, support modules.

input.content:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer, the (emerging) clap-free ops layer, the subcommand
model, and the two in-flight migrations toward that shape.

This is a living document — when the migrations land, update
the status tables here. Per-cycle design discussion stays in
`notes/chores-*.md`; the canonical "current shape" lives
here.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary, `vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for
managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace** — an app repo plus
a `.claude` bot-session repo whose commits cross-reference
each other via `ochid:` trailers. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

Each subcommand is a module under `src/`. `src/main.rs` owns
the clap command tree and dispatch; everything else is a
subcommand module or a shared support module.

## Module map

CLI edge:

- `src/main.rs` — `Cli` (`#[derive(Parser)]`) → `Commands`
  (`#[derive(Subcommand)]`) → per-variant dispatch arms.
  Also: `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand), `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion,
  and `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable CLI **leaves** and
  **bundles** (`account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`,
  `provision_bundle`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`,
  `use_template`) plus marker traits. The leaf/bundle
  composition patterns and the Flag-vs-Option classification
  are documented in
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (shared positional
  revision / `-R`,`--repo` list / `-n`,`--commits` /
  `--limit` / `-L`,`--no-label`) flattened by the read-only
  multi-repo subcommands (`chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`),
  plus the repo-iteration and revision-resolution helpers
  they share.

Ops-layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | ops-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Two layers: CLI args vs ops Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Ops layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters, not one merged "god context": the signature
documents what the op depends on. `Context` is the same
shape across subcommands; `XParams` differs per subcommand.

Rules for the ops layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running ops — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

What this is **not**: not a god `Context` (two params, not
one blob); not premature trait-based DI (concrete structs
until a second front-end forces it); not a crate split (same
crate, separate modules — promote to a workspace only when a
second consumer crate appears); not removing
`src/options_flags/` (leaves stay; only their consumers
change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Ops layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
4	layer, the (emerging) clap-free subcommand model.
5	
6	This is a living document and and this app is under continous
7	development and this and the entire repo will be changing and
8	time goes on. Discussions of "chores" reside in per-cycle
9	design discussion in `notes/chores-*.md` and `notes/todo.md`.
10	
11	## Overview
12	
13	`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
14	`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
15	with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
16	cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
17	of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
18	for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
19	the bot workflow.
20	
21	## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params
22	
23	Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
24	operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
25	embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
26	along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
27	nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
28	shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
29	fixing it.
30	
31	- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
32	  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
33	  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
34	  binary edge in each subcommand module.
35	- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
36	  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
37	  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
38	  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
39	  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
40	- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
41	  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
42	  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
43	  `args.account.account`.
44	
45	Two parameters in subcommads, the signature documents what
46	the op depends on. `Context` is the same across all subcommands
47	and `XxxParams` are the per subcommand specific parameters.
48	
49	Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
50	[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):
51	
52	1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
53	   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
54	   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
55	2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
56	   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
57	3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
58	   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
59	   nothing to stdout itself.
60	4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
61	   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
62	   recording sink.
63	5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
64	   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.
65	
66	## Module map
67	
68	CLI support types in main:
69	
70	- `src/main.rs`
71	  - `pub struct Cli` - clap Parser
72	  - `pub enum  Commands` - clap SubCommand and used in dispatch arms.
73	 
74	- CLI functions:
75	  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
76	  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
77	  subcommand)
78	  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
79	  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
80	  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)
81	
82	- CLI src/option_flags:
83	  - This directory contains every option for all subcommands
84	  as many are shared and thus reduces duplications. See
85	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
86	  for more details.
87	  - CLI reusable **leaves**
88	    `account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`,
89	    `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`
90	  
91	  - CLI reusable **bundles**
92	    - `provision_bundle`
93	
94	Subcommand layer scaffolding:
95	
96	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
97	  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
98	  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
99	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
100	- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
101	  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
102	  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.
103	
104	Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
105	struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:
106	
107	| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
108	| --- | --- | --- |
109	| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
110	| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
111	| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
112	| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
113	| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
114	| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
115	| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
116	| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
117	| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
118	| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
119	| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
120	| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |
121	
122	Support:
123	
124	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
125	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
126	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
127	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
128	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
129	  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
130	  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
131	  `sync` / `push`).
132	
133	## Subcommand model
134	
135	Adding a subcommand `x`:
136	
137	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
138	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
139	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
140	2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
141	   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
142	   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
143	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
144	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
145	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
146	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
147	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
148	   prose).
149	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
150	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
151	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
152	   and stays at this layer.
153	
154	
155	### Naming
156	
157	Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
158	capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
159	(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:
160	
161	- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
162	  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
163	  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
164	- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
165	  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
166	  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.
167	
168	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
169	
170	Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
171	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
172	`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
173	the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
174	0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
175	is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
176	[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
177	
178	| Subcommand | Status |
179	| --- | --- |
180	| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
181	| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
182	| `sync` | not started |
183	| `chid` | not started |
184	| `desc` | not started |
185	| `list` | not started |
186	| `show` | not started |
187	| `validate-desc` | not started |
188	| `fix-desc` | not started |
189	| `clone` | not started |
190	| `push` | not started |
191	| `symlink` | not started |
192	

... [45 lines truncated] ...

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

content.text:
Reviewed the edited `ARCHITECTURE.md`. The reorg (lead with the two-layer split, rename "ops layer" → "subcommand layer", restructure the module map into nested bullets, separate leaves vs bundles correctly) reads well. Here's what I'd tighten:

### Typos / wording
- **Line 6–9** (the "living document" para): `and and` → `and`; `continous` → `continuous`; `and time goes on` → `as time goes on`; and it's a run-on. Also `Discussions of "chores" reside in per-cycle design discussion in …` is doubled-up, and `notes/todo.md` isn't "chores". Suggest:
  > This is a living document; the app is under continuous development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in `notes/todo.md`.
- **Line 4**: "the (emerging) clap-free subcommand **model**" — should be "subcommand **layer**" to match the §"Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params" heading. ("Subcommand model" is the separate §about wiring a new subcommand — two different things now share that word.)
- **Line 45**: `subcommads` → `subcommands`. Also the sentence is a fragment — "Two parameters in subcommands; the signature documents what the op depends on." reads better with a semicolon, and `op` → `subcommand` for consistency with the rename.
- **Line 82**: `CLI src/option_flags:` → `src/options_flags` (directory has the `s`).
- **Lines 83–84**: "This directory contains **every option for all subcommands**" overclaims — the Migration B section right below says most subcommands still have inline `#[arg]` fields, and `CommonArgs` lives in `common.rs`. Suggest: "Reusable options/flags shared across subcommands (Migration B tracks what's been moved here so far)."
- **Line 72**: `pub enum  Commands` (double space) and `clap SubCommand` → `clap Subcommand` (trait name); line 73 has trailing whitespace; line 153–154 has a double blank line before `### Naming`.

### Structure
- **`### Naming` (line 155)** is now a subsection of `## Subcommand model`, but it's about the `Workspace`→`Context` / `XOptions`→`XParams` rename — it belongs under `## Two layers` (or as its own `##`). Suggest moving it right after the "Two layers" rules list.
- **Dropped "What this is *not*" paragraph.** The original had: not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob); not premature trait-based DI; **not a crate split — promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate appears**; not removing `src/options_flags/`. The crate-split point in particular is load-bearing guidance for a future contributor. I'd restore a trimmed version (one sentence). Your call — if you cut it for brevity, fine.
- Leading with "Two layers" before "Module map" means the reader meets `XArgs`/`Context`/`src/options_flags/` before the map says where they live. Defensible (it's the headline point) — just noting it; I wouldn't change it.

### Cross-doc consistency (needs a decision)
The doc now says **"subcommand layer"** everywhere, but:
- `notes/todo.md` #1 is titled **"Ops layer / CLI decoupling — sweep remaining subcommands"**
- `notes/chores-09.md` has **"## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)"** and the 0.44.0 section references "the design captured in Ops layer architecture"

The doc's own Migration A section (line 175) already references the todo item as "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" — which doesn't match the actual title. Two ways to resolve:
1. **Rename to "subcommand layer" everywhere** — update todo.md #1's title and the chores-09 references when I trim it next. (My lean — "subcommand layer" is the clearer name and you've committed the doc to it.)
2. **Keep "ops layer" as the cross-doc term**, revert the doc's renaming.

### Accuracy — all good
Leaves (8: `account`/`config`/`dry_run`/`private`/`push_retry`/`repo`/`scope`/`use_template`) and the lone bundle (`provision_bundle`) match `src/options_flags/`. The subcommand table and Migration A/B status are accurate.

Want me to apply the typo/wording fixes + move `### Naming` + restore a one-line "what this is not"? And which way on the "ops layer" vs "subcommand layer" naming — rename everywhere?

message.content: apply the fixes and rename to subcommand layer everywhere

content.content:
1	//! Shared platform handle passed to every subcommand operation.
2	//!
3	//! `Context` holds platform state that is the same across every
4	//! subcommand: today, the loaded `UserConfig`. Built once at CLI
5	//! startup and threaded through to the op layer.
6	//!
7	//! The shape is deliberately minimal — fields like project root,
8	//! progress sinks, etc. are added when a real consumer surfaces.
9	//! See `notes/chores-09.md > ## Ops layer architecture
10	//! (forward-looking)` and `## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)`.
11	
12	use crate::config::{self, UserConfig};
13	
14	/// Shared platform handle for subcommand operations.
15	///
16	/// - `user_config`: the loaded user config
17	///   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` or its discovered equivalent).
18	pub struct Context {
19	    pub user_config: UserConfig,
20	}
21	
22	impl Context {
23	    /// Build a `Context` by loading the user config from disk.
24	    pub fn load() -> Result<Self, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
25	        Ok(Self {
26	            user_config: config::load()?,
27	        })
28	    }

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer, the (emerging) clap-free subcommand model.

This is a living document and and this app is under continous
development and this and the entire repo will be changing and
time goes on. Discussions of "chores" reside in per-cycle
design discussion in `notes/chores-*.md` and `notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters in subcommads, the signature documents what
the op depends on. `Context` is the same across all subcommands
and `XxxParams` are the per subcommand specific parameters.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

## Module map

CLI support types in main:

- `src/main.rs`
  - `pub struct Cli` - clap Parser
  - `pub enum  Commands` - clap SubCommand and used in dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- CLI src/option_flags:
  - This directory contains every option for all subcommands
  as many are shared and thus reduces duplications. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  for more details.
  - CLI reusable **leaves**
    `account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`,
    `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`
  
  - CLI reusable **bundles**
    - `provision_bundle`

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

## Module map

CLI support types in main:

- `src/main.rs`
  - `pub struct Cli` - clap Parser
  - `pub enum  Commands` - clap SubCommand and used in dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- CLI src/option_flags:
  - This directory contains every option for all subcommands
  as many are shared and thus reduces duplications. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  for more details.
  - CLI reusable **leaves**
    `account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`,
    `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`
  
  - CLI reusable **bundles**
    - `provision_bundle`

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI support types in main:

- `src/main.rs`
  - `pub struct Cli` - clap Parser
  - `pub enum  Commands` - clap SubCommand and used in dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- CLI src/option_flags:
  - This directory contains every option for all subcommands
  as many are shared and thus reduces duplications. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  for more details.
  - CLI reusable **leaves**
    `account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`,
    `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`
  
  - CLI reusable **bundles**
    - `provision_bundle`

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- CLI src/option_flags:
  - This directory contains every option for all subcommands
  as many are shared and thus reduces duplications. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  for more details.
  - CLI reusable **leaves**
    `account`, `config`, `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`,
    `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`
  
  - CLI reusable **bundles**
    - `provision_bundle`

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.

  `sync` / `push`).

   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |

| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every op runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.
