# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into op bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Op bodies read `params.account`, never
  `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into subcommand bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Subcommand bodies read `params.account`,
  never `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI

83	  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
84	  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.
85	
86	## Module map
87	
88	CLI layer:
89	
90	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
91	  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
92	  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
93	    dispatch arms.
94	 
95	- CLI functions:
96	  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
97	  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
98	  subcommand)
99	  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
100	  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
101	  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)
102	
103	- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
104	  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
105	  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
106	  far. See
107	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
108	  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
109	    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
110	  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
111	- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
112	  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
113	  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
114	  and revision-resolution helpers they share.
115	
116	Subcommand layer scaffolding:
117	
118	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
119	  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
120	  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
121	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
122	- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
123	  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
124	  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.
125	
126	Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
127	struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:
128	
129	| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
130	| --- | --- | --- |
131	| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
132	| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
133	| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
134	| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
135	| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
136	| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
137	| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
138	| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
139	| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
140	| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
141	| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
142	| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |
143	
144	Support:
145	
146	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
147	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
148	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
149	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
150	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
151	  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
152	  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
153	  `sync` / `push`).
154	
155	## Subcommand model
156	
157	Adding a subcommand `x`:
158	
159	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
160	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
161	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
162	2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
163	   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
164	   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
165	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
166	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
167	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
168	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
169	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
170	   prose).
171	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
172	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
173	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
174	   and stays at this layer.
175	
176	
177	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
178	
179	Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
180	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
181	`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
182	the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
183	0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
184	is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
185	[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
186	
187	| Subcommand | Status |
188	| --- | --- |
189	| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
190	| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
191	| `sync` | not started |
192	| `chid` | not started |
193	| `desc` | not started |
194	| `list` | not started |
195	| `show` | not started |
196	| `validate-desc` | not started |
197	| `fix-desc` | not started |
198	| `clone` | not started |
199	| `push` | not started |
200	| `symlink` | not started |
201	
202	Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
203	consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
204	`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
205	beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.
206	
207	## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`
208	
209	Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
210	fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
211	`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
212	completer) exactly once. See
213	[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
214	for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
215	Flag-vs-Option classification.
216	
217	State today:
218	
219	- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
220	  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
221	  `use_template`, `config`).
222	- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
223	  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
224	  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
225	  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
226	  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
227	  form).
228	- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
229	  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
230	  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
231	  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
232	  point for converting one.
233	
234	## See also
235	
236	- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
237	  per-subcommand usage.
238	- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
239	  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
240	- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
241	  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
242	- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
243	  including both migrations.
244	- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
245	  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into subcommand bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Subcommand bodies read `params.account`,
  never `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand
   returns a structured result; CLI formats it, a library
   writes nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
 
- CLI functions:
  - `cli_with_banner()` (walks the command tree adding
  the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every
  subcommand)
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion
  - `bm_track` (a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command)

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.


## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams)`, adding an
`XParams` flat struct + `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams` at
the binary edge. `init` was done as the worked example in
0.44.0; the rest follow the same shape. The live checklist
is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)` |
| `finalize` | partial — `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` (no `Context`; `into_opts` rather than `From`) |
| `sync` | not started |
| `chid` | not started |
| `desc` | not started |
| `list` | not started |
| `show` | not started |
| `validate-desc` | not started |
| `fix-desc` | not started |
| `clone` | not started |
| `push` | not started |
| `symlink` | not started |

Out of scope for the early ports (deferred until a real
consumer surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs
`println!`, the `ProgressSink`, and any `Context` fields
beyond workspace root + `UserConfig`.

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The "CommonArgs sweep"
  todo item folds these into the leaf model (and drops the
  repeatable `-R`/`--repo` in favor of the `--scope` path
  form).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `finalize` / `symlink`** — still mostly
  inline `#[arg]` fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into subcommand bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)** — `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
  structs that flatten leaves from `src/options_flags/`. Own
  the clap metadata for `--help` and completion. Live at the
  binary edge in each subcommand module.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)** — `Context` (shared platform:
  workspace root, loaded `UserConfig`, later a progress
  sink) plus a per-subcommand `XParams` plain struct (flat
  fields, owns its values, `Default`). Entrypoint shape:
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XParams) -> Result<…>`.
- **Boundary conversion** — `impl From<&XArgs> for XParams`,
  one contained site per subcommand, where leaf nesting is
  unpacked once. Subcommand bodies read `params.account`,
  never `args.account.account`.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand
   returns a structured result; CLI formats it, a library
   writes nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

Not a god `Context` (two params, not one blob), not premature
trait-based DI (concrete structs until a second front-end
forces it), not a crate split (same crate, separate modules —
promote to a workspace only when a second consumer crate
appears), and not removing `src/options_flags/` (leaves stay;
only their consumers change shape).

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig`; built once at startup via `Context::load()`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | `FinalizeArgs::into_opts(log)` → `FinalizeOpts` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XArgs`

2. Add a variant `X(x::XArgs)` to `Commands` in

4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add

5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

- Restored a one-line "not a god `Context` / not premature DI / **not a crate split** / not removing `src/options_flags/`" paragraph after the rules list.

- `src/context.rs` — module doc: `threaded through to the op layer` → `subcommand layer`; pointer `notes/chores-09.md > ## Ops layer architecture …` → `ARCHITECTURE.md`.

Grep confirms no `ops?[- ]layer` / `op body` left anywhere except **`notes/chores-09.md`** — which I'm trimming next (its "Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)" section gets replaced with a stub pointer to `ARCHITECTURE.md`, and the 0.44.0-section cross-ref + the `[80]` anchor in todo.md get repointed then).

680	
681	## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)
682	
683	Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
684	port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
685	example for the design captured in
686	[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
687	above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.
688	
689	### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above
690	
691	Written before this cycle, the design section uses
692	`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:
693	
694	- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
695	  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
696	  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
697	  Doubly overloaded.
698	- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
699	  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
700	  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
701	  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.
702	
703	The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
704	this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
705	is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
706	during a future close-out so the codebase has one
707	naming.
708	
709	### Why init
710	
711	`args.account.account` ergonomics — the trigger for the
712	whole design — lives here; init also has the deepest
713	leaf nesting (account / repo / scope / private /
714	template / push retry). If the pattern survives init,
715	sync / chid / push / clone port trivially.
716	
717	### In scope
718	
719	- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding the
720	  workspace root path + loaded `UserConfig`. Built once
721	  at startup.
722	- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat plain
723	  struct (owns its values) + `impl From<&InitArgs> for
724	  InitParams`.
725	- `src/init.rs`: port `plan_init` and any other
726	  init-side `pub fn` entrypoints to
727	  `fn (&Context, &InitParams)`. Body reads
728	  `params.account` instead of `args.account.account`.
729	- `src/main.rs`: build `Context` once at startup; the
730	  init dispatch arm passes `(&ctx, &(&args).into())`.
731	- `src/init/tests.rs`: pure-unit tests construct
732	  `Context` + `InitParams` directly (no clap
733	  `try_parse_from` plumbing); integration paths still
734	  exercise the clap edge.
735	
736	### Out of scope (deferred)
737	
738	- Sweep across sync / chid / push / clone / etc.
739	  Each is its own later cycle.
740	- Per chores-09 "five rules": typed errors, returned
741	  outcomes (vs `println!`), `ProgressSink`. Keep this
742	  cycle to the structural split only.
743	- `Context` fields beyond workspace root + user
744	  config. Defer until a real consumer surfaces.
745	
746	### Pre-commit checklist
747	
748	Standard: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --
749	-D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
750	--locked`, retest.
751	
752	### Edits
753	
754	- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
755	- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding
756	  `user_config: UserConfig` + `Context::load()` ctor;
757	  declared `mod context;` in `src/main.rs`.
758	- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat struct
759	  + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production
760	  default sets `create_symlink: true`); declared
761	  `mod params; pub use params::InitParams;` in
762	  `src/init.rs`.
763	- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` signature changed
764	  from `init(args: &InitArgs, create_symlink: bool)`
765	  to `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`
766	  (`create_symlink` folded into `InitParams`).
767	  Internal helpers (`plan_init`, `plan_from_url`,
768	  `plan_from_path`, `plan_from_bare_name`,
769	  `plan_remote`, `plan_local`, `build_plan`,
770	  `create_por`, `create_dual`, `push_repo`,
771	  `run_remote_step`) all switched from
772	  `args: &InitArgs` to `params: &InitParams`. Body
773	  reads `params.account` instead of
774	  `args.account.account` (and similarly across all
775	  leaf nestings). `args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)`
776	  call sites collapse to `&params.config` since the
777	  resolve runs at the boundary now. `cfg` inside
778	  `pub fn init` switched from `config::load()?` to
779	  `&ctx.user_config`.
780	- `src/main.rs`: `mod context;` added; init dispatch
781	  builds `Context::load()` then converts
782	  `InitParams::from(&init_args)` and calls
783	  `init::init(&ctx, &params)`.
784	- `src/test_helpers.rs`: `Fixture` and `FixturePor`
785	  builders updated — load `Context`, build
786	  `InitParams::from(&args)`, override
787	  `params.create_symlink = false`, then call
788	  `init(&ctx, &params)`. Imports updated to bring
789	  `Context` and `InitParams` into scope.
790	- `src/init/tests.rs`: every `plan_init(&args, ...)`
791	  call site rewritten to
792	  `plan_init(&InitParams::from(&args), ...)`. The two
793	  `plan_init(&args_for(...), ...)` sites split across

803	  reflect "init done, sweep remaining."

## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)

Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
example for the design captured in
[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.

### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above

Written before this cycle, the design section uses
`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:

- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
  Doubly overloaded.
- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.

The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
during a future close-out so the codebase has one
naming.

### Why init

`args.account.account` ergonomics — the trigger for the
whole design — lives here; init also has the deepest
leaf nesting (account / repo / scope / private /
template / push retry). If the pattern survives init,
sync / chid / push / clone port trivially.

### In scope

- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding the
  workspace root path + loaded `UserConfig`. Built once
  at startup.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat plain
  struct (owns its values) + `impl From<&InitArgs> for
  InitParams`.
- `src/init.rs`: port `plan_init` and any other
  init-side `pub fn` entrypoints to
  `fn (&Context, &InitParams)`. Body reads
  `params.account` instead of `args.account.account`.
- `src/main.rs`: build `Context` once at startup; the
  init dispatch arm passes `(&ctx, &(&args).into())`.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: pure-unit tests construct
  `Context` + `InitParams` directly (no clap
  `try_parse_from` plumbing); integration paths still
  exercise the clap edge.

### Out of scope (deferred)

- Sweep across sync / chid / push / clone / etc.
  Each is its own later cycle.
- Per chores-09 "five rules": typed errors, returned
  outcomes (vs `println!`), `ProgressSink`. Keep this
  cycle to the structural split only.
- `Context` fields beyond workspace root + user
  config. Defer until a real consumer surfaces.

### Pre-commit checklist

Standard: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --
-D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
--locked`, retest.

### Edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding
  `user_config: UserConfig` + `Context::load()` ctor;
  declared `mod context;` in `src/main.rs`.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat struct
  + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production
  default sets `create_symlink: true`); declared
  `mod params; pub use params::InitParams;` in
  `src/init.rs`.
- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` signature changed
  from `init(args: &InitArgs, create_symlink: bool)`
  to `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`
  (`create_symlink` folded into `InitParams`).
  Internal helpers (`plan_init`, `plan_from_url`,
  `plan_from_path`, `plan_from_bare_name`,
  `plan_remote`, `plan_local`, `build_plan`,
  `create_por`, `create_dual`, `push_repo`,
  `run_remote_step`) all switched from
  `args: &InitArgs` to `params: &InitParams`. Body
  reads `params.account` instead of
  `args.account.account` (and similarly across all
  leaf nestings). `args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)`
  call sites collapse to `&params.config` since the
  resolve runs at the boundary now. `cfg` inside
  `pub fn init` switched from `config::load()?` to
  `&ctx.user_config`.
- `src/main.rs`: `mod context;` added; init dispatch
  builds `Context::load()` then converts
  `InitParams::from(&init_args)` and calls
  `init::init(&ctx, &params)`.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`: `Fixture` and `FixturePor`
  builders updated — load `Context`, build
  `InitParams::from(&args)`, override
  `params.create_symlink = false`, then call
  `init(&ctx, &params)`. Imports updated to bring
  `Context` and `InitParams` into scope.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: every `plan_init(&args, ...)`
  call site rewritten to
  `plan_init(&InitParams::from(&args), ...)`. The two
  `plan_init(&args_for(...), ...)` sites split across
  multiple lines for readability. Tests of `InitArgs`
  (parse/defaults) untouched — those exercise the
  clap edge.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section + the
  `## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)` plan
  section.
- `notes/todo.md`: drop the cycle's `## In Progress`
  entry; add `## Done` entry; rewrite the
  `Ops layer / CLI decoupling` `## Todo` item to
  reflect "init done, sweep remaining."

1	# Chores-09.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..08.md;
5	09 starts here because chores-07 (0.42.0 cycle, 600+ lines) and
6	chores-08 (0.41.1 cycle, 1500+ lines) are both already large; the
7	init-clone-refactor rebase landing is a natural new-file
8	boundary.
9	
10	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
11	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
12	
13	## init-clone-refactor rebase landing (0.42.0-4.7)
14	
15	Rebased main (0.42.0-0..-4.6) onto init-clone-refactor at its
16	0.41.1 close-out tip (`slvprlpw`). Chids preserved; ochid
17	trailers in the rebased commits stayed structurally valid (the
18	.claude pairings they point at had been WC-only during the icr
19	divergence; -4.7 itself re-establishes the .claude side).
20	
21	### What needed real work
22	
23	- **ttzwvpoq (0.42.0-1):** full `Scope` enum lifted wholesale
24	  from pre-rebase main; `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `is_bot_only`
25	  and `Single` / `parse_scope` since icr's bundle design keeps
26	  them dormant.
27	- **mvusyowm (0.42.0-2):** icr's bundle-flatten `InitArgs` +
28	  `ScopeOption`/`ScopeKind` already supersedes mvusyowm's
29	  parser-switch + `Option<Scope>` design; took icr wholesale
30	  across all 5 init.rs conflict regions. The cycle's narrative
31	  intent (custom `--scope` parser accepting paths) becomes
32	  dormant on the icr base; the `Scope::Single` variant survives
33	  for future use.
34	- **kyurxpnu (0.42.0-4.5):** CLAUDE.md substep references merged
35	  (lulqxovr's icr-side decomposition pointer + kyurxpnu's
36	  external `substep-protocol.md` / `jj-revsets.md` links) into
37	  a single continuation paragraph under `### Versioning`.
38	- **Reference renumbering** — cycle's `[72]`/`[73]`/`[74]`
39	  collided with icr's chores-08 anchors: `[72]→[76]`,
40	  `[73]→[77]`, `[74]→[78]`, plus self-refs in chores-07's
41	  -4.5 and -4.6 sections.
42	
43	### Precautions taken (most unnecessary in retrospect)
44	
45	- `main-2` local duplicate bookmark — never read.
46	- `gca-icr-main` common-ancestor marker — never referenced.
47	- `rslv-commit` cursor bookmark — handy as a navigation
48	  aid during cascade, not load-bearing (`@-` works).
49	- `../vc-x1-main` + `../vc-x1-icr` reference clones — useful
50	  for content lookup at specific chids; replaceable with
51	  `jj log -r <chid> --patch` against the local repo.
52	- Filesystem snapshots `vc-x1-20260505-1`, `vc-x1-20260506-1`
53	  — never restored from.
54	- `~/vc-x1-rebase-status.md` scratch file — useful for
55	  resuming across sessions, but ~200 lines was over-detailed;
56	  a 30-line cursor + decisions log would have sufficed.
57	
58	### What was load-bearing
59	
60	- `jj op restore` for local rewinds.
61	- origin's canonical pre-rebase main remaining intact until
62	  the explicit force-push at the end (the only fallback that
63	  mattered).
64	- jj's chid-preservation across rebase: ochid trailers stay
65	  valid pointers without manual fixup.
66	- Per-commit cargo cycle (fmt/clippy/test/install --locked)
67	  to verify each squash kept the chain buildable.
68	
69	### Distilled recipe for next time
70	
71	1. `jj rebase` of the divergent chain onto the new base.
72	2. Per conflicted commit: `jj new <commit>`, edit conflicts,
73	   cargo cycle, `jj squash`.
74	3. After cascade clears: cargo cycle at tip, `jj git push
75	   --bookmark main`.
76	4. `.claude` side: `vc-x1 push` to author the paired commit
77	   once main converges with origin.
78	
79	Skip the duplicate bookmarks, reference clones, and
80	filesystem snapshots unless a specific reason emerges. The
81	op-log + origin are the safety net.
82	
83	### Edits
84	
85	- `notes/chores-09.md`: this file (new).
86	- `notes/todo.md`: ladder marker flips
87	  (-4.6 (current)→(done), add -4.7 (current)); add `-4.7`
88	  Done entry referenced as `[79]`; add `[79]:` ref target;
89	  move pre-0.42.0 Done entries to `notes/done.md`.
90	- `notes/done.md`: append migrated 0.40/0.41 Done entries.
91	- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.42.0-4.6 → 0.42.0-4.7.
92	
93	## 0.42.0 close-out
94	
95	Cycle closed at -4.7 (init-clone-refactor rebase landing)
96	rather than completing the originally planned full
97	`--scope` sweep. What shipped, what deferred, and why.
98	
99	### Shipped
100	
101	- 0.42.0-0 plan + version bump + new chores-07.md
102	- 0.42.0-1 `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`)
103	- 0.42.0-2 custom CLI parser + `init --scope` retrofit
104	- 0.42.0-3 `sync --scope` retrofit (drop -R, add -s)
105	- 0.42.0-4.5 substep protocol + jj revsets cheatsheet
106	- 0.42.0-4.6 init-clone-refactor recovery + post-mortem
107	- 0.42.0-4.7 init-clone-refactor rebase landing
108	
109	### Deferred to future cycles
110	
111	Originally-planned -4 / -5 / -6 / -7 substeps — the
112	`--scope` sweep across the remaining subcommands — moved
113	back to `notes/todo.md > ## Todo`. Design references stay
114	at chores-07 [76]:
115	
116	- `vc-x1 push --scope` (was -4; pivoted into substep
117	  protocol + icr work).
118	- `vc-x1 finalize --scope` (was -5).
119	- `vc-x1 clone --scope` (was -6).
120	- `Single(_)` end-to-end dogfood validation (was -7).
121	
122	Plus already-scheduled but co-deferred items: `vc-x1
123	validate-desc / fix-desc --scope` and the CommonArgs
124	sweep across `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show`.
125	
126	### Narrative shift
127	
128	The cycle started as a `--scope` sweep but pivoted at
129	-4.5:
130	
131	1. Substep protocol formalization (-4.5) emerged as
132	   needed before further substep work.
133	2. init-clone-refactor recovery (-4.6) surfaced as
134	   higher-priority than `push --scope` once the icr
135	   branch was located.
136	3. Rebasing icr onto the cycle's tip (-4.7) became the
137	   natural pivot point. The bot thinks continuing
138	   `push --scope` past -4.7 would have lengthened an
139	   already-pivoted cycle past the point of useful
140	   narrative coherence; closing here and reopening
141	   fresh later produces cleaner history.
142	
143	### Edits
144	
145	- `notes/todo.md`: 0.42.0 ladder removed from `## In
146	  Progress`; consolidated `--scope` continuation TODO
147	  entry added; four existing scope-related entries
148	  updated to drop "0.42.0 cycle" claims; `0.42.0 cycle
149	  close-out` Done line added; `[81]` reference target
150	  added.
151	- `notes/chores-09.md`: this subsection (new).
152	- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.42.0-4.7 → 0.42.0.
153	
154	## Design notes: bot-data + multi-user updates (0.42.1)
155	
156	Documentation-only follow-on to the 0.42.0 close-out.
157	Forward-looking design captures for multi-user
158	collaboration, multi-bot vendor support, and bot-repo
159	scaling thresholds. No code change.
160	
161	### Edits
162	
163	- `notes/bot-data-formats.md`: new file. Format-agnostic
164	  principle, dual-repo merits-based defense,
165	  vendor-subdir layout
166	  (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`), multi-bot in
167	  one repo, format versioning, flat-to-vendor migration,
168	  `.claude` → `.bot` rename, viewer layer, open
169	  questions.
170	- `notes/forks-multi-user.md`: four new subsections
171	  (bot-repo size and scaling thresholds;
172	  monotonic-growth asymmetry; mitigation menu; tracking
173	  trigger). One new subsection on URL-shaped ochid for
174	  per-user repos (link-rot mitigations: project-side
175	  mirroring, cryptographic stapling, CI-enforced live
176	  ochid). Cross-ref to `bot-data-formats.md` added in
177	  intro.
178	- `notes/todo.md`: replace multi-user TODO entry with
179	  `forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through`;
180	  add `[82]` / `[83]` refs to the two design docs
181	  (slight extension of the existing
182	  `chores-NN.md#anchor` reference style for whole-file
183	  pointers).
184	- `README.md`: TOC entry +
185	  `## Thoughts for the future` section pointing at
186	  `notes/forks-multi-user.md`. Reader chain: README →
187	  forks-multi-user → bot-data-formats.
188	- `notes/chores-09.md`: this subsection (new).
189	- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.42.0 → 0.42.1.
190	
191	## Test-module extraction (0.43.0)
192	
193	Multi-step cycle to extract `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
194	`mod integration_tests` where present) from oversized
195	production files into sibling-submodule layout. Pure
196	mechanical reshape — no behavior change, no API change.
197	Each test still reaches private items via
198	`use super::*;`.
199	
200	### Goals
201	
202	- Shrink production files so the actual code is easier
203	  to navigate (`init.rs` is 2576 lines today, ~1093 of
204	  which are tests).
205	- Establish a layout shape that future cycles can keep
206	  using (further splitting into `cli.rs` / `ops.rs` per
207	  the ops-layer architecture, etc.).
208	- Done as one cycle so the four files end up
209	  consistent rather than half-converted across multiple
210	  cycles.
211	
212	### Per-file shape
213	
214	Non-mod.rs layout (Rust 2018+ idiom): the production
215	file keeps its top-level path; a sibling directory holds
216	children. `src/X.rs` and `src/X/` coexist — Rust resolves
217	`mod foo;` declared in `src/X.rs` to `src/X/foo.rs`.
218	
219	```
220	src/X.rs               ← production code (unchanged path)
221	src/X/
222	  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
223	  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
224	                         integration_tests (push, sync)
225	```
226	
227	In `src/X.rs` after extraction:
228	
229	```rust
230	// ...production code...
231	
232	#[cfg(test)]
233	mod tests;
234	
235	#[cfg(test)]
236	mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
237	```
238	
239	In `src/X/tests.rs`:
240	
241	```rust
242	//! Unit tests for the X module.
243	
244	use super::*;
245	// ...test fns...
246	```
247	
248	**Why non-mod.rs.** The `mod.rs` style names every
249	module's entry point identically — fuzzy finders show a
250	column of `mod.rs` rows that the reader must
251	disambiguate by directory. Multiple `tests.rs` files
252	don't have the same pain (the file is leaf content; the
253	directory tells the reader which module's tests they
254	are). Modern Rust (`cargo new`, RFC 2126) defaults to
255	non-mod.rs for exactly this reason.
256

506	## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)
507	
508	Design target for subsequent cycles: separate clap-aware
509	parsing from subcommand operation logic so future front-ends
510	(e.g. a TUI for bot-conversation exploration, or library
511	embedding) call the same core without retrofitting. Captures
512	the conclusion of the 0.42.0-4.7 side discussion about
513	`args.account.account` ergonomics — renaming / accessor
514	shortcuts were rejected as hiding the architecture mismatch
515	rather than fixing it.
516	
517	### Goals
518	
519	- Subcommand bodies access flat fields (`opts.account`), not
520	  nested clap shapes (`args.account.account`).
521	- `src/options_flags/` leaves remain the single source of
522	  truth for flag definition (parser, help, completer).
523	- Per-subcommand `-h` stays bundle-specific.
524	- Tab completion (`vc-x1 init <TAB>`, `--account=<TAB>`)
525	  keeps working via clap_complete + dynamic completers.
526	- The ops layer is callable without clap as a dependency
527	  (today's `plan_init(args: &InitArgs, ...)` is not).
528	
529	### Two layers, three structures per subcommand
530	
531	- **CLI layer (clap-aware):** `InitArgs` / `CloneArgs` / etc.
532	  `#[derive(Args)]` types that flatten leaves from
533	  `src/options_flags/`. Own clap metadata for `-h` and
534	  completion. Live at the binary edge.
535	- **Ops layer (clap-free):** `InitOptions` / `CloneOptions`
536	  — plain structs, flat fields, `Default`. Plus `Workspace`,
537	  the shared platform handle (workspace root, loaded
538	  `UserConfig`, optional progress sink). Entrypoints are
539	  `ops::init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions) -> Result<InitOutcome, InitError>`.
540	- **Boundary conversion:** `From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions`
541	  — one contained site per subcommand where leaf nesting is
542	  unpacked. Op bodies never see `args.xxx.xxx`.
543	
544	### `Workspace` ("context") vs per-op options
545	
546	- **`Workspace`:** shared platform every op runs against —
547	  workspace root, loaded user config, optional progress
548	  sink. Same shape across subcommands.
549	- **`InitOptions`** etc.: per-op input; shape differs by
550	  subcommand.
551	
552	Two parameters, not a single merged "god context." The
553	signature `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`
554	documents what the op depends on; a merged `Context`
555	containing every possible field would let any op silently
556	read any field with no signature-level visibility.
557	
558	If `Workspace` itself grows, escape valves (defer until
559	needed):
560	
561	- Split into `Workspace` (paths + config, read-only, cheap)
562	  vs `Session` (progress / cancellation, mutable). Most ops
563	  want `&Workspace`; long-running ops also accept
564	  `&Session`.
565	- Trait-based DI (`fn init<W: HasConfig + HasFs>(...)`) for
566	  multiple front-ends with genuinely different platform
567	  surfaces. Heavy in Rust; not the default.
568	
569	### Five rules for the ops layer
570	
571	1. **Plain options, flat fields, `Default`.** No clap types
572	   in `InitOptions`. Domain values like `RepoSelector` are
573	   fine (they're domain types); leaf wrappers like
574	   `RepoOption` are not.
575	2. **Typed errors.** `enum InitError` (likely via
576	   `thiserror`), not `Box<dyn Error>`. A TUI matches
577	   variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
578	   `Box<dyn Error>` discards exactly the information a GUI
579	   most needs.
580	3. **Returned outcomes, not `println!`.** Each op returns a
581	   structured result; CLI formats it for stdout, TUI
582	   populates panels. Library writes nothing to stdout
583	   itself.
584	4. **Progress via a sink.** Long-running ops accept an
585	   optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` (or
586	   `mpsc::Sender<Event>`). CLI installs a stderr sink; TUI
587	   a status-bar sink; tests a recording sink. `log` /
588	   `tracing` covers diagnostics but is lossy for structured
589	   progress.
590	5. **No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops.**
591	   Everything an op needs goes in `Workspace` or
592	   `*Options`. CLI resolves cwd/env once at startup and
593	   builds the handle. This is what lets a multi-window TUI
594	   drive multiple workspaces without cross-contamination.
595	
596	### Completion stays at the clap layer
597	
598	- Static structure (`-h`, subcommand layout, flag presence):
599	  clap derive on `InitArgs` + leaves. Unchanged from today.
600	- Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>` against
601	  accounts in user config): `ArgValueCompleter` attached to
602	  the leaf in `src/options_flags/`. Already enabled via
603	  `clap_complete`'s `unstable-dynamic` feature. The leaf is
604	  the right home — completion is a clap-aware concern with
605	  no place in `InitOptions`.
606	
607	Ops layer stays clap-free; leaf layer stays clap-aware.
608	Completion drives nothing in the ops layer.
609	
610	### The contained wart
611	
612	`From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions` walks leaf nesting once
613	per subcommand:
614	
615	```rust
616	impl From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions {
617	    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
618	        Self {
619	            target: a.target.clone(),
620	            account: a.account.account.clone(),
621	            scope: a.scope.scope,
622	            private: a.provision.private.private,
623	            // ...
624	        }
625	    }
626	}
627	```
628	
629	Verbose but contained: one site per subcommand. Adding a new
630	flag becomes a two-edit change (clap struct + options struct
631	+ one conversion line). Accepted price of decoupling; bodies
632	are flat thereafter.
633	
634	### What this is *not*
635	
636	- **Not a god `Context`.** Two parameters
637	  (`&Workspace`, `&XOptions`), not one merged blob.
638	- **Not premature trait-based DI.** Concrete `Workspace` +
639	  `*Options` structs until a second front-end forces
640	  generalization.
641	- **Not a crate split.** Same crate, separate modules
642	  (e.g. `src/ops/init.rs` vs CLI edge). Promote to a
643	  workspace only when a second consumer crate appears.
644	- **Not removing `src/options_flags/`.** Leaves stay; only
645	  their consumers change shape.
646	
647	### Migration sketch
648	
649	The bot thinks the safest first move is converting one
650	subcommand end-to-end as the worked example before any
651	sweep. `init` is the largest surface; `sync` or `chid` would
652	be a lighter proof of concept.
653	
654	1. Introduce `ops::Workspace` (paths + `UserConfig` only; no
655	   progress sink yet). Wire it into one subcommand.
656	2. Introduce `XOptions` for the chosen first subcommand;
657	   write `From<&XArgs> for XOptions`; port the body to
658	   `&Workspace, &XOptions`.
659	3. Add typed errors + returned outcome for that subcommand.
660	4. Sweep remaining subcommands; each is a contained step.
661	5. (Later) introduce `ProgressSink` when a TUI need actually
662	   surfaces, or a long-running op wants structured progress.
663	
664	The bot thinks the right shape of `Workspace` matters more
665	than the right shape of `InitError` — defer error-type and
666	outcome-type design until step 1 has shaken out.
667	
668	### Open questions
669	
670	- `Workspace` carries the progress sink from day one even
671	  with no consumer? The bot's guess is no — leave it out and
672	  add when a real long-running op forces it; adding later is
673	  mechanical.
674	- `XOptions` owns values (`String`) or borrows (`&str`)? The
675	  bot's guess is owns — simpler, matches the programmatic-
676	  caller path (build then call). Defer borrow optimization
677	  until benchmarks call for it.
678	- Crate split timing — defer until a second consumer crate
679	  appears.
